design_changes: NL translation deferred to v2.0 — all v1.0 interactions are structured/typed
Minimum version enforcement on receptors with sunset policy
Non-interference proof required for persona lattice
Session types adopted as receptor formalism
rationale: EMA-I fills a genuine architectural gap — no spec in the Atrahasis stack defines how humans or external systems interact with the system. The receptor model formalized via session types is scientifically grounded, the persona projection model maps to solved problems, and the structured translation path is deterministic and composable. NL deferral to v2.0 removes the biggest risk. Impact is high because every governance, marketplace, operational, and developer interaction depends on this layer.
